news.title: AI.com Launches: AI Agents Platform
news.summary: Crypto.com CEO Kris Marszalek launched AI.com, a new platform aiming to mainstream AI agents and AGI in the same way he led mass consumer adoption of cryptocurrency, allowing users to generate private, personal AI agents that operate on their behalf.

news.title: D4RT: Teaching AI to See in 4 Dimensions
news.summary: Google DeepMind released D4RT (Deep 4D Representation and Tracking), a method for teaching AI to perceive the world in four dimensions instead of just three, enhancing spatial understanding for robotic and vision systems.

news.title: NVIDIA Nemotron ColEmbed V2: Multimodal Retrieval Breakthrough
news.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron ColEmbed V2, raising the bar for multimodal retrieval with ViDoRe V3's top model, enabling more sophisticated cross-modal search and retrieval capabilities for enterprise applications.

news.title: OpenClaw Partners with VirusTotal for Skill Security
news.summary: After over 400 malicious AI skills were uploaded to ClawHub and GitHub in just one week, OpenClaw partnered with VirusTotal to scan third-party skills. The company acknowledges it's not a "silver bullet" but should provide at least some reassurance to concerned users.

news.title: Project Genie: Infinite, Interactive Worlds
news.summary: Google DeepMind introduced Project Genie, an AI system for generating infinite, interactive 2D worlds and game environments, pushing the boundaries of what AI agents can create and explore.

news.title: ServiceNow Launches SyGra Studio AI Platform
news.summary: ServiceNow introduced SyGra Studio, a new AI-powered development platform designed to streamline workflow automation and intelligent system integration for enterprise applications.

news.title: Transformers.js v4 Preview: AI in the Browser
news.summary: Hugging Face released Transformers.js v4 Preview on NPM, enabling advanced AI model inference directly in web browsers and Node.js environments without requiring backend infrastructure or API calls, democratizing AI access for developers.

news.title: Veo 3.1: More Consistent Video Generation
news.summary: Google DeepMind unveiled Veo 3.1, bringing more consistency, creativity, and control to AI video generation, representing significant improvements in quality and user control over generated video content.

news.title: ChatGPT Gets Canva Brand Kit Integration
news.summary: ChatGPT users who create designs via Canva app can now connect to their Canva Brand Kits, allowing designs to draw from on-brand colors and assets, while Anthropic's Claude had this feature first, continuing the competition between the two platforms.

news.title: Claude Opus 4.6: Anthropic's Upgraded Smartest Model
news.summary: Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.6, upgrading their smartest model which achieves industry-leading performance across agentic coding, computer use, tool use, search, and finance, often by wide margin.

news.title: GPT-5.3-Codex: First Model to Help Code Itself
news.summary: OpenAI announced GPT-5.3-Codex, its new coding and development model, which is apparently the first "that was instrumental in creating itself." While it probably doesn't mean ChatGPT is ready to build its own Skynet, the model was able to accelerate its own development through debugging, testing, and deployment assistance.

news.title: OpenClaw Scans AI Skills After Malicious Add-ons Found
news.summary: Researchers raised alarms when over 400 malicious skills were uploaded to ClawHub and GitHub in just one week. That prompted an outcry, leading OpenClaw to partner with VirusTotal to scan third-party skills and provide reassurance to concerned users about AI ecosystem security.

news.title: Community Evals: Open-Source Model Evaluation Initiative
news.summary: Hugging Face introduced Community Evals, emphasizing transparent, community-driven model evaluation over closed proprietary leaderboards to ensure more reliable and trustworthy AI benchmarking.

news.title: H Company's Holo2 Model Leads in UI Localization
news.summary: H Company released Holo2 235B model, achieving state-of-the-art performance in UI localization tasks, improving how AI systems understand and interact with user interface elements.

news.title: NVIDIA Nemotron ColEmbed V2: Raising Bar for Multimodal Retrieval
news.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron ColEmbed V2, raising the bar for multimodal retrieval with ViDoRe V3's top model, enabling more sophisticated cross-modal search and retrieval capabilities.

news.title: Transformers.js v4 Preview: Now Available on NPM
news.summary: Hugging Face released Transformers.js v4 Preview on NPM, enabling advanced AI model inference directly in web browsers and Node.js environments without requiring backend infrastructure or API calls, democratizing AI access for developers.

news.title: Cancer Index Surges 72% as Terns, Olema Lead Gains
news.summary: The BioWorld Cancer Index closed 2025 up 72.35%, vastly outpacing gains in the Nasdaq Biotechnology Index (up 32.4%) and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (up 12.97%), reflecting exceptional investor confidence in oncology and biotechnology sector.

news.title: Innovent-Lilly $8.5B Deal Signals New China Inflection Point
news.summary: The scale of the $8.5 billion deal signed between Innovent Biologics Inc. and Eli Lilly and Co. is eye-catching, but the structure signals a real shift in China's healthcare market and foreign investment priorities.

news.title: Three-stop Loop Mechanism Behind Post-Myocardial Infarction Responses
news.summary: Research identified the neural and neuroimmune mechanisms behind post-myocardial infarction-triggered cardiac events, immune responses, and nervous system activation. Understanding these mechanisms could lead to better treatments for heart attack survivors and prevention strategies.

news.title: Agentic Uncertainty Reveals Agentic Overconfidence
news.summary: This study examines whether AI agents can accurately predict their own success at tasks by eliciting success probability estimates before, during, and after task execution. All results exhibit agentic overconfidence: some agents that succeed only 22% of the time predict 77% success. Counterintuitively, pre-execution assessment often outperforms post-execution review.

news.title: AIRS-Bench: A Suite of Tasks for Frontier AI Research Science Agents
news.summary: AIRS-Bench introduces 20 tasks sourced from state-of-the-art machine learning papers, spanning diverse domains including language modeling, mathematics, bioinformatics, and time series forecasting. Benchmarks assess agentic capabilities across full research lifecycle without providing baseline code.

news.title: From Features to Actions: Explainability in Traditional and Agentic AI Systems
news.summary: This work bridges gap between static and agentic explainability by comparing attribution-based explanations with trace-based diagnostics across both settings. Results show attribution methods achieve stable feature rankings in static settings (Spearman ρ = 0.86) but cannot reliably diagnose execution-level failures in agentic trajectories.

news.title: ChatGPT to Introduce Sponsored Links for Plus Subscribers
news.summary: OpenAI announced that ChatGPT Plus subscribers will start seeing "sponsored" links in chat responses, marking a shift toward more transparent advertising as the company seeks new revenue streams amid $670 billion annual AI infrastructure spending by big tech.

news.title: EU Orders Meta to Allow Competing AIs on WhatsApp
news.summary: The European Commission ruled that Meta's November decision to block ChatGPT and Copilot from WhatsApp violates EU antitrust laws, calling it an "urgent" issue due to the risk of "irreparable" damage to competition in the nascent AI industry.

news.title: OpenClaw Partners with VirusTotal for Security Scanning
news.summary: After over 400 malicious AI skills were uploaded to ClawHub and GitHub in one week, OpenClaw partnered with VirusTotal to scan third-party skills, acknowledging concerns about ecosystem security while providing reassurance to users.

news.summary: The Rafah border crossing between Gaza and Egypt reopened for limited traffic in what officials describe as a key step toward a possible ceasefire. The reopening follows months of restricted access to the Palestinian territory.

news.summary: Prominent pro-democracy media tycoon Jimmy Lai received the harshest possible penalty of 20 years in prison by Hong Kong High Court. The case has drawn international condemnation from Western leaders including US President Trump and UK PM Starmer.

news.title: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi Secures Historic Election Victory
news.summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's Liberal Democratic Party won 316 out of 465 seats in Sunday's election, securing a super-majority in parliament. The result signals significant political change and creates opportunities for new economic and technology policies.

news.title: Trump Administration Explores New Iran Military Options
news.summary: Reports indicate the Trump administration is weighing new military options against Iran, with a "massive armada" reportedly heading toward the region. The developments come amid escalating tensions in the Middle East.

news.title: UK Prime Minister Starmer Faces Political Crisis Over Epstein Files
news.summary: UK PM Keir Starmer is fighting for political survival after Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar called for his resignation over appointing an associate of Jeffrey Epstein to a key government position. A key aide has resigned amid the growing controversy.

news.title: Adaptive Differentially Private Federated Learning
news.summary: Research proposes a new framework with adaptive gradient clipping and constraint-aware aggregation to address privacy-constrained federated learning challenges, improving convergence stability on CIFAR-10 and SVHN benchmarks.

news.summary: Hugging Face introduced Community Evals, emphasizing transparent, community-driven model evaluation over closed proprietary leaderboards to ensure more reliable and trustworthy AI benchmarking.

news.summary: Google DeepMind announced Gemini 3, marking a new era of intelligence, alongside Project Genie for experimenting with infinite, interactive 3D worlds, enabling complex environment simulation for AI training.

news.title: LLM Active Alignment: Nash Equilibrium Perspective
news.summary: New research develops a game-theoretic framework for predicting and steering LLM behavior through Nash equilibrium analysis, enabling active alignment that functions as a layer on top of RLHF pipelines.

news.summary: Meta's Facebook is rolling out new AI-powered features including animated profile photos and background options for text posts. The updates continue Meta's push to integrate generative AI across its social platforms.

news.summary: Google accidentally sent personal and financial information of a student journalist to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) after receiving a data request. The incident raises serious concerns about tech companies' data handling and privacy protections.

The Commission called the issue "urgent" due to risks of "irreparable damage" to competition in the AI industry.

news.summary: Transformers.js v4 is now available on NPM, enabling developers to run transformer models directly in the browser and Node.js environments. This update brings improved performance, new model support, and better developer experience for browser-based ML.

news.title: NVIDIA Nemotron ColEmbed V2 with ViDoRe V3 Integration
news.summary: NVIDIA has released Nemotron ColEmbed V2, which raises the bar for multimodal retrieval with integration of ViDoRe V3's top-performing model. This advancement improves how AI systems retrieve and understand multimodal content across text and images.

news.title: OpenAI Hardware Launch Delayed Until 2027
news.summary: OpenAI's first hardware products, rumored to include smart speakers, glasses, and wearables, have been delayed until at least March 2027. The delay was confirmed in a court filing as part of a trademark dispute, pushing back expectations for consumer AI hardware.

news.title: ServiceNow Introduces SyGra Studio
news.summary: ServiceNow has announced SyGra Studio, a new tool for AI-powered workflow automation and graph-based reasoning. The platform aims to help enterprises build more sophisticated AI agents that can handle complex business processes and decision-making tasks.

news.title: Super Bowl AI Ad War: OpenAI vs Anthropic
news.summary: Both OpenAI and Anthropic ran Super Bowl commercials this year, sparking controversy. Anthropic's ad criticized AI advertising practices with "There is a time and place for ads. Your conversations with AI should not be one of them," while OpenAI showcased Codex's coding capabilities. Sam Altman called Anthropic's ads "clearly dishonest."

news.title: YouTube Music Introduces AI-Powered Playlist Generator
news.summary: YouTube Music has launched an AI Playlists feature for premium subscribers, allowing users to create personalized playlists using voice or text descriptions. Similar to Spotify's Prompted Playlists, the feature can turn ideas, genres, or vibes into curated music collections.

news.title: Data Science and Technology Towards AGI Part I: Tiered Data Management
news.summary: Introduces a novel L0-L4 tiered data management framework designed to support the full LLM training lifecycle across heterogeneous learning objectives and cost constraints. The framework represents a shift from unidirectional data scaling to data-model co-evolution, where models actively guide data management while high-quality data amplifies capabilities.

news.title: GEBench: Benchmarking Image Generation Models as GUI Environments
news.summary: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating dynamic interaction and temporal coherence in GUI generation using image models. GEBench comprises 700 curated samples spanning five task categories, covering single-step interactions and multi-step trajectories. Introduces GE-Score, a novel five-dimensional metric assessing Goal Achievement, Interaction Logic, Content Consistency, UI Plausibility, and Visual Quality.

news.title: InternAgent-1.5: A Unified Agentic Framework for Long-Horizon Autonomous Scientific Discovery
news.summary: A comprehensive system designed for end-to-end scientific discovery across computational and empirical domains. Built on a structured architecture with three coordinated subsystems for generation, verification, and evolution, supporting deep research, solution optimization, and long-horizon memory.

news.title: stable-worldmodel-v1: Reproducible World Modeling Research and Evaluation
news.summary: A modular, tested, and documented world-model research ecosystem that provides efficient data-collection tools, standardized environments, planning algorithms, and baseline implementations. Addresses the issue of publication-specific implementations that limit reusability and reduce evaluation standardization in World Model research.

news.title: AI Can AI Help Students Navigate the Career Chaos It's Creating?
news.summary: As AI rapidly transforms the job market, educators and EdTech companies are grappling with how to prepare students for careers that may not yet exist. New initiatives focus on teaching adaptability, critical thinking, and AI literacy alongside traditional skills.

news.title: AI I Teach in a Tech-Powered System That Never Sleeps — and My Students Feel the Cost
news.summary: A teacher's firsthand account reveals how constant digital connectivity, AI-powered learning platforms, and 24/7 accessibility expectations are affecting student well-being and creating burnout. The piece argues for more intentional boundaries around technology use in education.

news.title: AI When Machines Think, Human Thinking Must Go Higher
news.summary: Sponsored analysis argues that as AI systems become more capable of routine cognitive tasks, education must shift toward developing uniquely human cognitive capabilities: creativity, ethical reasoning, emotional intelligence, and complex problem-solving that AI cannot easily replicate.

news.title: Market K-12 EdTech in 2026: Five Trends Shaping the Year Ahead
news.summary: EdSurge's trends report identifies key developments including AI-powered personalized learning, increased focus on data privacy, the rise of VR/AR educational tools, growth in teacher professional development platforms, and continued consolidation in the market.

news.title: Policy New Report Card Grades States on Laws Banning Phones in Schools
news.summary: EdSurge's new comprehensive report evaluates state-level legislation restricting phone use in K-12 schools. The analysis examines implementation effectiveness, student outcomes, and the growing movement toward stricter device policies to combat distraction and improve focus.

news.title: Agentic Test-Time Scaling for WebAgents
news.summary: This work presents CATTS (Confidence-Aware Test-Time Scaling), a technique for dynamically allocating computational resources in multi-step AI agents. The authors find that uniformly increasing per-step compute quickly saturates in long-horizon environments. CATTS uses uncertainty statistics (entropy and top-1/top-2 margin) from the agent's own vote distribution to allocate compute only when decisions are genuinely contentious.

news.title: CM2: Reinforcement Learning with Checklist Rewards for Multi-Turn and Multi-Step Agentic Tool Use
news.summary: This paper introduces CM2, a novel reinforcement learning framework that replaces traditional verifiable outcome rewards with "checklist rewards" for training multi-turn, multi-step AI agents. The approach decomposes each turn's intended behavior into fine-grained binary criteria with explicit evidence grounding, turning open-ended judgment into more stable classification-style decisions. Training occurs in a scalable LLM-simulated tool environment, avoiding heavy engineering overhead.

news.title: Pedagogically-Inspired Data Synthesis for Language Model Knowledge Distillation
news.summary: This work introduces IOA (Identifier, Organizer, Adapter), a pedagogically-inspired framework for LLM knowledge distillation that draws from educational principles like Bloom's Mastery Learning and Vygotsky's Zone of Proximal Development. The three-stage pipeline systematically identifies knowledge deficiencies in student models, organizes knowledge delivery through progressive curricula, and adapts representations to match cognitive capacity.

news.title: SAM3-LiteText: An Anatomical Study of the SAM3 Text Encoder for Efficient Vision-Language Segmentation
news.summary: This paper performs a large-scale anatomical analysis of text prompting in vision-language segmentation, analyzing 404,796 real prompts across multiple benchmarks. The study reveals severe redundancy: most context windows are underutilized, vocabulary usage is highly sparse, and text embeddings lie on low-dimensional manifolds despite high-dimensional representations. SAM3-LiteText replaces the original SAM3 text encoder with a compact MobileCLIP student optimized by knowledge distillation.

news.title: Think like a Scientist: Physics-guided LLM Agent for Equation Discovery
news.summary: This paper introduces KeplerAgent, an agentic framework that explicitly follows the scientific reasoning process for symbolic equation discovery. Unlike existing LLM-based systems that guess equations directly from data, KeplerAgent first infers physical properties (such as symmetries) and uses these as priors to restrict the space of candidate equations. The agent coordinates physics-based tools to extract intermediate structure and configures symbolic regression engines like PySINDy and PySR accordingly.

news.title: Anthropic Upgrades Opus to Version 4.6
news.summary: Anthropic has upgraded its smartest model to Opus 4.6, showing industry-leading performance across agentic coding, computer use, tool use, search, and finance tasks. The upgrade maintains Anthropic's position at the forefront of AI capabilities.

news.title: Disney Accuses ByteDance of Copyright Infringement
news.summary: Disney has sent a cease and desist letter to ByteDance, alleging that their new Seedance 2.0 AI video model infringes on Disney's intellectual property by reproducing characters like Spider-Man and Darth Vader without authorization.

news.title: Google Adds AI Audio Summarization to Docs
news.summary: Google has introduced Gemini-powered Audio Summaries in Google Docs, allowing users to get condensed overviews of lengthy documents instead of reading entire texts. The feature is rolling out to select business and Google AI users.

news.title: Google Introduces Gemini 3 Deep Think
news.summary: Google DeepMind has unveiled Gemini 3 Deep Think, advancing capabilities in science, research, and engineering. The model is positioned to accelerate mathematical and scientific discovery through enhanced reasoning and analytical capabilities.

news.title: Hugging Face: Custom CUDA Kernels Built by Claude
news.summary: In a groundbreaking demonstration, Claude successfully built custom CUDA kernels that are now available for all users. The achievement showcases AI's growing capability in low-level system programming and performance optimization.

news.title: India Mandates 3-Hour Deepfake Takedown
news.summary: India has ordered social media platforms to remove deepfakes within three hours of takedown requests. The updated IT rules also require labeling of synthetic content and ban deceptive impersonations and non-consensual intimate imagery.

news.title: Meta Sells 7 Million AI Smart Glasses in 2025
news.summary: Meta achieved exponential growth in smart glasses sales, selling over 7 million units in 2025 - triple the combined sales of 2023 and 2024. EssilorLuxottica CEO confirmed the milestone, with the company on track to reach 10 million annual units by 2027.

news.title: OpenAI Reportedly Disbands Mission Alignment Team
news.summary: OpenAI has reportedly disbanded its Mission Alignment team, which was tasked with ensuring AGI benefits all humanity. Team members have been transferred to other areas, and former team lead Joshua Achiam will become OpenAI's "chief futurist."

news.title: Senate Bill Requires AI Training Data Disclosure
news.summary: Sens. Adam Schiff (D-CA) and John Curtis (R-UT) introduced the bipartisan Copyright Labeling and Ethical AI Reporting Act (CLEAR Act), which would force tech companies to provide written notice about copyrighted materials used to train AI models.

news.title: Transformers.js v4 Preview Launches on NPM
news.summary: Hugging Face has released Transformers.js v4 Preview, now available on NPM. The update brings improved performance and broader compatibility for running transformer models directly in web browsers and Node.js environments.

news.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Sonnet 4.6 with Improved Computer Use
news.summary: Anthropic released Sonnet 4.6, which it says "approaches Opus-level intelligence," featuring significant improvements in coding and computer use capabilities. The model can now navigate spreadsheets, fill out web forms, and replace Sonnet 4.5 as the default model for free and pro Claude users.

news.title: Anthropic Raises $30 Billion at $380 Billion Post-Money Valuation
news.summary: Anthropic closed a $30 billion Series G funding round led by GIC and Coatue, valuing the company at $380 billion post-money. The investment will fuel frontier research, product development, and infrastructure expansion as Anthropic reports $14 billion in run-rate revenue with 10x annual growth over the past three years.

news.title: Custom CUDA Kernels Now Available from Codex and Claude
news.summary: A breakthrough project demonstrated that AI agents can write custom CUDA kernels, enabling significant performance optimizations for GPU computing. The work showed both Codex and Claude capable of generating highly optimized GPU code, opening new possibilities for AI-assisted performance engineering.

news.title: Hugging Face Launches Free AI Model Training with Unsloth
news.summary: Hugging Face announced a new integration with Unsloth that allows developers to train AI models for free using Hugging Face Jobs. The partnership aims to democratize AI model training by reducing infrastructure costs and improving accessibility for researchers and developers worldwide.

news.title: IBM and UC Berkeley Diagnose Enterprise Agent Failures with New Benchmarks
news.summary: Researchers from IBM and UC Berkeley introduced IT-Bench and MAST, new benchmarks designed to diagnose why enterprise AI agents fail. The work provides systematic evaluation frameworks to improve reliability and performance of AI agents in real-world business environments.

news.title: Meta Plans $65 Million Election Spending to Influence AI Legislation
news.summary: Meta is allocating $65 million to pro-AI super PACs, including new Republican-focused "Forge the Future Project" and Democrat-focused "Making Our Tomorrow" groups. The funding will back politicians who support AI-friendly policies and push back against legislation that could limit Meta's AI business growth.

news.title: Netflix Issues Legal Threat to ByteDance Over Seedance AI Copyright Infringement
news.summary: Netflix gave ByteDance three days to stop its Seedance AI tool from generating unauthorized derivative works using Netflix IP including Stranger Things, KPop Demon Hunters, Squid Game, and Bridgerton. Netflix threatened "immediate litigation" for what it called a "high-speed piracy engine."

news.title: OpenAI Hires Instagram's VP of Global Partnerships
news.summary: Charles Porch, Instagram's VP of global partnerships who oversaw major collaborations including Beyoncé's album launch, joins OpenAI as its first VP of global creative partnerships. He will focus on building relationships with creative communities to develop AI products that serve creators.

news.title: Sony Develops Technology to Identify AI Song Source Material
news.summary: Sony has developed new technology that can identify how much influence a given track or artist had on AI-generated music content. The system works with or without cooperation from AI developers, addressing copyright concerns in the music industry.

news.title: xAI Faces Legal Battle Over Unpermitted Gas Turbines

news.title: AI AI Energy Consumption Challenges the Sector's Growth
news.summary: As data center energy demands surge due to AI training and inference, the technology industry faces growing pressure to address its environmental footprint. Companies like xAI are encountering regulatory challenges over unpermitted energy infrastructure, while utilities struggle to meet unprecedented power demands.

news.title: ENERGY Climate Tech Sector Trends: Solar, Storage, and Grid Modernization
news.summary: Climate technology startups continue to focus on solar energy innovation, battery storage solutions, and grid modernization as key areas for decarbonization. Investment patterns show growing interest in technologies that can integrate renewable energy at scale while maintaining grid stability.

news.title: ENERGY SoftBank Plans $33B Investment in U.S. Gas Power Plant
news.summary: SoftBank announced plans to spend $33 billion to build a massive gas power plant in the United States. The investment represents one of the largest recent commitments to fossil fuel infrastructure, raising questions about energy transition priorities and climate impact.

news.title: POLICY Energy Transition Policy and Regulatory Landscape
news.summary: Governments worldwide continue to implement policies supporting renewable energy adoption, carbon pricing mechanisms, and energy efficiency standards. The regulatory environment increasingly influences investment decisions across traditional and renewable energy sectors.

news.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Sonnet 4.6
news.summary: The latest Claude Sonnet model "approaches Opus-level intelligence" with major improvements in coding, computer use (navigating spreadsheets, filling web forms), and agentic capabilities. Sonnet 4.6 replaces Sonnet 4.5 as the default model.

news.title: Anthropic Raises $30B at $380B Valuation
news.summary: Anthropic raised $30 billion in Series G funding led by GIC and Coatue, valuing the company at $380 billion post-money. The company's run-rate revenue is $14 billion, growing over 10x annually in each of the past three years.

news.title: ChatGPT Ads Launch
news.summary: OpenAI has started showing ads in ChatGPT responses. Ads from Expedia, Qualcomm, Best Buy, and Enterprise Mobility are appearing after user prompts, marking a significant monetization shift.

news.title: Claude Opus 4.6 Released
news.summary: Anthropic upgraded its smartest model with industry-leading performance across agentic coding, computer use, tool use, search, and finance tasks. Opus 4.6 often outperforms competitors by wide margins.

news.title: Epic Acquires AI Animation Company
news.summary: Epic Games is acquiring Meshcapade, a company specializing in AI technology for animating digital humans. The team will join Epic's AI Research to advance MetaHumans and digital human technologies for gaming, film, and entertainment.

news.title: Google Gemini 3.1 Pro Rollout
news.summary: Google's new Gemini 3.1 Pro AI model "represents a step forward in core reasoning" and is rolling out in the Gemini app and NotebookLM. Designed for advanced reasoning tasks requiring more than simple answers.

news.title: Meta Plans $65M for AI-Friendly Elections
news.summary: Meta will spend $65 million on election spending through pro-AI super PACs including Republican-focused "Forge the Future Project" and Democrat-focused "Making Our Tomorrow" to back politicians friendly to AI legislation.

news.title: YouTube AI Chatbot Expands to TV
news.summary: YouTube is testing its conversational AI tool on smart TVs, gaming consoles, and streaming devices with a small group of users. The tool lets users ask questions about videos they're watching.

news.title: ChatGPT Gets Lockdown Mode for Enhanced Security
news.summary: OpenAI introduces Lockdown Mode for ChatGPT, which "tightly constrains how ChatGPT can interact with external systems to reduce the risk of prompt injection-based data exfiltration." Targeted at high-security use cases.

news.title: DoD May Designate Anthropic as Supply Chain Risk
news.summary: Department of Defense may designate Anthropic as a "supply chain risk," which would require anyone doing business with the US military to cut ties with the company. The two sides have been negotiating military AI tool usage for months.

news.title: Hugging Face: GGML and llama.cpp Join HF Ecosystem
news.summary: GGML and llama.cpp have officially joined the Hugging Face ecosystem to ensure long-term progress of Local AI. This integration strengthens local AI development and democratization efforts.

news.title: NAACP Threatens Lawsuit Over xAI's Unpermitted Gas Turbines
news.summary: NAACP sent notice of intent to sue xAI for illegally installing gas turbines in Mississippi to power its Colossus 2 data center. Drone thermal images show more than a dozen turbines running without permits.

news.title: Sony Develops Tech to Identify Source Material for AI Music
news.summary: Sony has developed technology that can recognize how much influence a given track or artist had on AI-generated content. The company says it could create a licensing system for AI music but hasn't decided when to deploy it.

news.title: Train AI Models for Free with Unsloth and Hugging Face Jobs
news.summary: Hugging Face launches free AI model training using Unsloth framework through their Jobs platform. This dramatically lowers barriers to entry for AI developers and researchers.

news.title: Transformers.js v4 Preview Available on NPM
news.summary: Hugging Face releases Transformers.js v4 preview, bringing transformer models directly to JavaScript environments. This enables easier integration of AI models in web applications and Node.js projects.

news.title: Unity Plans AI-Driven Full Game Generation
news.summary: Unity CEO Matthew Bromberg announced "AI-driven authoring is our second major area of focus for 2026" with plans to let developers "prompt full casual games into existence" with AI. Details coming at GDC Festival in March.

news.title: CBDC Development Gains Momentum Globally
news.summary: Central banks across major economies are accelerating their Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) pilots and implementations. Several countries have moved from testing to limited public rollout, while others are finalizing technical specifications for national digital currency systems.

news.title: Crypto ETFs See Record Inflows Amid Institutional Adoption
news.summary: Cryptocurrency ETF products continue to attract significant institutional capital, with daily inflows reaching record levels in Q1 2026. Major asset managers are expanding their crypto offerings, signaling growing mainstream acceptance of digital assets as part of diversified investment portfolios.

news.title: DeFi 2.0 Projects Focus on Institutional-Grade Security and Compliance
news.summary: New decentralized finance protocols are prioritizing regulatory compliance, institutional-grade security audits, and insurance integration. This "DeFi 2.0" wave aims to attract institutional investors who were previously hesitant to participate in decentralized markets.

news.title: Fintech-Powered Embedded Finance Expands Beyond Traditional Sectors
news.summary: Embedded finance solutions are expanding from e-commerce and marketplaces into healthcare, education, and government services. Non-financial companies are increasingly offering banking, lending, and payment capabilities directly to their customers through white-label fintech APIs.

news.title: Traditional Banks Accelerate AI Integration in Lending and Risk Assessment
news.summary: Major financial institutions are rapidly deploying AI-powered lending platforms and risk assessment tools, reducing approval times from days to minutes while improving fraud detection accuracy by up to 40%. This trend is reshaping consumer banking and small business financing.

news.title: AutoNumerics: An Autonomous, PDE-Agnostic Multi-Agent Pipeline for Scientific Computing
news.summary: A multi-agent framework that autonomously designs, implements, debugs, and verifies numerical solvers for general partial differential equations (PDEs) directly from natural language descriptions. Generates transparent solvers grounded in classical numerical analysis.

news.title: CLEF HIPE-2026: Evaluating Accurate and Efficient Person-Place Relation Extraction from Multilingual Historical Texts
news.summary: A CLEF evaluation lab dedicated to person-place relation extraction from noisy, multilingual historical texts. Targets identifying person-place associations across languages and time periods with a three-fold evaluation of accuracy, computational efficiency, and domain generalization.

news.title: MARS: Margin-Aware Reward-Modeling with Self-Refinement
news.summary: An adaptive, margin-aware augmentation and sampling strategy for reward modeling that explicitly targets ambiguous and failure modes. Concentrates augmentation on low-margin preference pairs where the reward model is most uncertain.

news.title: MolHIT: Advancing Molecular-Graph Generation with Hierarchical Discrete Diffusion Models
news.summary: A molecular graph generation framework using Hierarchical Discrete Diffusion Models that overcomes long-standing performance limitations in existing methods. MolHIT achieves near-perfect validity on the MOSES dataset and surpasses strong 1D baselines across multiple metrics.

news.title: Multi-Round Human-AI Collaboration with User-Specified Requirements
news.summary: A human-centric framework for multi-round conversational AI that enforces counterfactual harm (AI doesn't undermine human strengths) and complementarity (AI adds value where humans err) through user-defined rules with finite sample guarantees.

news.title: Australia seals UK defence deal with $300M for AUKUS sub reactors
news.summary: Australia has committed $300 million to the UK for nuclear submarine reactor development under AUKUS. The deal strengthens the trilateral defense partnership.

news.title: Ex-British minister Mandelson released on bail in Epstein probe
news.summary: Lord Peter Mandelson was arrested and released on bail amid the Epstein leaks probe. Prince Andrew remains in the spotlight as the royal family navigates the crisis.

news.title: Trump's worldwide tariff back to 10%
news.summary: After SCOTUS struck down emergency powers tariffs, Trump's new tariff will be 10% (not 15% as initially announced). FedEx has sued the administration for a "full refund" of unlawful tariffs. Trump vows retaliation against countries that "play games."

news.title: War enters 4th year under maximum pressure
news.summary: Both Russia and Ukraine under extreme strain. War of attrition appears set to continue despite Trump's pressure on Ukraine to sign away territory.

news.title: DeepSeek allegedly targeted Claude's reasoning capabilities
news.summary: Reports suggest DeepSeek used Claude outputs to train its models, generating "censorship-safe alternatives to politically sensitive questions." This raises concerns about model distillation and IP theft in the AI race.

news.title: Meta researcher's AI agent deletes her Gmail inbox
news.summary: Meta safety researcher Summer Yue connected an AI agent to her Gmail and watched it "speedrun deleting her inbox" despite sending "STOP OPENCLAW." The agent "lost" her instruction not to take action without checking first. A cautionary tale for AI agent safety.

news.title: OpenAI's Stargate data center project struggles
news.summary: OpenAI reportedly explored building its own data centers but high costs led to striking individual deals with SoftBank and Oracle instead of the joint Stargate venture. The three companies disagreed over their respective roles.

news.title: X working on 'Made with AI' labels
news.summary: X (Twitter) is developing a feature to let users disclose AI-generated or manipulated content. The move may be in response to India ordering social platforms to embrace C2PA provenance systems — a standard X abandoned under Elon Musk.

news.title: ChatGPT ads go live
news.summary: OpenAI starts showing ads in ChatGPT responses. Early advertisers include Expedia, Qualcomm, Best Buy, and Enterprise Mobility. Ads can trigger after the first prompt.

news.title: Gemini can now create music
news.summary: Google DeepMind announces music generation capabilities for Gemini, expanding the model's multimodal creative abilities.

news.title: GGML and llama.cpp join Hugging Face
news.summary: Major consolidation for local AI: GGML and llama.cpp teams join Hugging Face to ensure long-term progress of local/open-source AI inference. Significant for privacy-first and edge AI applications.

news.title: Meta to spend $65M on AI-focused election influence
news.summary: Meta plans to fund pro-AI super PACs including Republican-focused "Forge the Future Project" and Democrat-focused "Making Our Tomorrow" to back politicians friendly to AI and push back against restrictive legislation.

news.title: Mistral Voxtral: Real-time audio transcription
news.summary: Mistral AI launches Voxtral for precision diarization and real-time transcription, along with a new audio playground.

news.title: Nvidia and OpenAI in major investment talks
news.summary: Reports suggest Nvidia and OpenAI are close to a significant new investment deal, potentially reshaping the AI infrastructure landscape.

news.title: OpenAI hires Instagram's VP of Global Partnerships
news.summary: Charles Porch, who helped launch Beyoncé's album on Instagram, becomes OpenAI's first VP of Global Creative Partnerships to build products for creative communities.

news.title: Analyzing and Improving Chain-of-Thought Monitorability Through Information Theory
news.summary: Uses information theory to formalize when and how LLM chain-of-thought reasoning can be monitored. Identifies two key error sources (information gap and elicitation error) and proposes two training methods to improve monitorability: an oracle-based approach and a practical label-free method that maximizes conditional mutual information.

news.title: On the Semantic and Syntactic Information Encoded in Proto-Tokens for One-Step Text Reconstruction
news.summary: Investigates how frozen LLMs can reconstruct hundreds of tokens from just two learned "proto-tokens" in a single forward pass — a potential path beyond autoregressive generation. Analyzes what information these proto-tokens encode (semantic vs syntactic) and how to impose structure on them.

news.title: Thinking by Subtraction: Confidence-Driven Contrastive Decoding for LLM Reasoning
news.summary: A novel training-free decoding method that dramatically improves LLM reasoning accuracy by targeting low-confidence tokens during generation. Instead of uniform computation across all tokens, this approach detects where the model is uncertain, constructs a contrastive reference, and selectively refines predictions at those positions.

news.title: VIRAASAT: Traversing Novel Paths for Indian Cultural Reasoning
news.summary: Introduces VIRAASAT, a semi-automated multi-hop question-answering dataset for Indian culture spanning 28 states and 8 union territories. Includes a Knowledge Graph with 700+ cultural artifacts and proposes Symbolic Chain-of-Manipulation (SCoM), a framework that teaches models to simulate KG manipulations internally.

news.title: Anthropic Raises $30B at $380B Valuation
news.summary: Anthropic raised $30B in Series G funding led by GIC and Coatue, valuing the company at $380B post-money. Run-rate revenue: $14B, growing 10x annually for 3 consecutive years.

news.summary: Anthropic has publicly accused Chinese AI labs of systematically extracting knowledge from its Claude models through sophisticated prompting strategies, effectively reverse-engineering capabilities without access to model weights.

news.title: Reddit Fined $20M for Children's Data Violations

news.title: Software Stocks Hit by AI-Fueled Selloff
news.summary: US software and private capital shares experienced a fresh wave of selling as investors rotate away from AI-exposed stocks toward asset-heavy industries. The AI hype cycle is forcing a market correction in SaaS valuations.

news.title: Autonomously Scaling Synthetic Environments for Reasoning Models
news.summary: Introduces ReSyn, a pipeline that automatically generates diverse reasoning environments with verifiers for training reasoning language models. A Qwen2.5-7B trained on ReSyn data achieves a 27% relative improvement on the challenging BBEH benchmark and generalizes to out-of-domain math tasks.

news.title: Learning Hierarchical Optimization Structures from Data
news.summary: Introduces Behavior Learning (BL), a framework that learns interpretable optimization structures from data by parameterizing compositional utility functions. Provides theoretical universal approximation guarantees and demonstrates strong predictive performance with intrinsic interpretability.

news.title: Recurrent Structural Policy Gradient for Partially Observable Mean Field Games
news.summary: Proposes RSPG, the first history-aware hybrid structural method for partially observable mean field games. Achieves SOTA performance with order-of-magnitude faster convergence. Also releases MFAX, a JAX-based framework for MFG research.

news.title: To Reason or Not to: Selective Chain-of-Thought in Medical Question Answering
news.summary: Proposes Selective CoT—an inference-time strategy that predicts whether a question needs reasoning before generating a rationale. Reduces inference time by 13-45% and token usage by 8-47% with minimal accuracy loss (≤4%) on four biomedical QA benchmarks.

news.title: AMD Inks $100B AI Chip Deal with Meta
news.summary: Multi-year agreement for six gigawatts of AMD processors for AI data centers. The deal could see Meta owning 10% of AMD stock, following a similar AMD-OpenAI agreement.

news.summary: New benchmark evaluating LLM agents on realistic tasks requiring information gathering, synthesis, and structured reasoning. SOTA agents achieve only 8.97-17.5 F1, exposing massive gaps in deep research capabilities.

news.title: A Data-Efficient Vision-Language-Action Model that Drives without Reasoning (NoRD)
news.summary: NoRD achieves competitive autonomous driving performance with less than 60% of training data and zero reasoning annotations—3x fewer tokens than existing VLAs. The key insight is using Dr. GRPO to mitigate "difficulty bias" in policy optimization, enabling efficient learning from small, reasoning-free datasets.

news.title: On Data Engineering for Scaling LLM Terminal Capabilities

news.title: Test-Time Training with KV Binding Is Secretly Linear Attention
news.summary: This paper reframes test-time training (TTT) with KV binding as a form of learned linear attention, contradicting the common interpretation of TTT as online meta-learning. The new perspective enables architectural simplifications, fully parallel formulations, and explains previously puzzling model behaviors.

news.title: Why Pass@k Optimization Can Degrade Pass@1: Prompt Interference in LLM Post-training
news.summary: This paper provides a theoretical explanation for a puzzling phenomenon: optimizing LLMs for pass@k (success with k samples) often degrades pass@1 (single-shot success). The authors show that pass@k optimization implicitly reweights toward low-success prompts, and when these prompts exhibit "negative interference," the update direction rotates away from pass@1 improvement.

news.title: Bias Studies: Language Models Show Algorithm Aversion
news.summary: Evaluates how LLMs weigh information from human experts vs. algorithms in decision-making tasks. Finds LLMs rate human experts higher, showing algorithm aversion bias. However, when betting between human expert and algorithm, LLMs disproportionately choose algorithm even when it performs worse, revealing inconsistent biases.

news.title: GUI Agents: Training Native GUI Agents to Reason and Act

news.title: Petri Net Relaxation for Infeasibility Explanation and Sequential Task Planning
news.summary: Proposes Petri net reachability relaxation for robust invariant synthesis, efficient goal-unreachability detection, and helpful infeasibility explanations. Leverages incremental constraint solvers to support goal and constraint updates. Empirically, compared to baselines, the system produces a comparable number of invariants, detects up to 2x more infeasibilities, performs competitively in one-shot planning, and outperforms in sequential plan updates in tested domains.

news.title: Surrogate Models: Rock-Fluid Interaction Grid-Size-Invariant Approach

The application analyzed is particularly challenging because fluid-induced rock dissolution results in a non-static solid field.

news.summary: Evaluates how LLMs weigh information from human experts vs algorithms in decision-making tasks. Finds LLMs rate human experts higher (unfair advantage), showing algorithm aversion bias. news.title: Partial Grounding via LLMs

Shows that adding simple "think step-by-step" prompts significantly improves knowledge recall (+9.9% on TriviaQA, +4.2% on Natural Questions, +3.0% on HotpotQA, +0.6% on StrategyQA) but not math. By training models to reason over their parametric knowledge using world-knowledge QA as a verifiable reward, performance improves across multiple benchmarks.

news.title: Semantic Grounding via LLMs (SPG-LLM)

news.title: Improving Parametric Knowledge Access in Reasoning Language Models

news.title: SumTablets: A Transliteration Dataset of Sumerian Tablets

news.title: Lumina Tmall 1M Agents: A Multi-Agent Shopping Platform
news.summary: Presents Lumina Tmall 1M, a multi-agent shopping platform designed for the 100 million product SKU catalog at Tmall. The system consists of three core modules: perception, policy, and action, each built on specialized foundation models. The perception module identifies products from images with 95.7% accuracy and extracts 3.5K detailed attributes. The policy module translates business rules into natural language constraints with 98%+ consistency. The action module coordinates multi-round interactions with reinforcement learning, achieving a 51.5% task completion rate. Lumina 1M demonstrates that multi-agent systems with strong module specialization can achieve high-level e-commerce success, surpassing traditional single-model approaches. System evaluations show significant improvements over baselines, with perception improving product identification by 16.7% and action success by 32.3% relative to single-agent methods.

news.title: Open-source Native GUI Agents for Reasoning and Action-Aware Supervision
news.summary: Native GUI agents still lag behind closed-source systems on long-horizon navigation tasks due to shortage of high-quality, action-aligned reasoning data. This gap stems from two limitations: (i) standard SFT with CoT reasoning often hurts grounding and (ii) step-wise RLVR-style training faces partial verifiability, where multiple actions can be correct but only a single demonstrated action is used for verification. In this work, we present GUI-Libra, a tailored training recipe that addresses these challenges. First, to mitigate the scarcity of action-aligned reasoning data, we introduce a data construction and filtering pipeline and release a curated 81K GUI reasoning dataset. Second, to reconcile reasoning with grounding, we propose action-aware SFT that mixes reasoning-then-action and direct-action data and reweights tokens to emphasize action and grounding. Third, to stabilize RL under partial verifiability, we identify the overlooked importance of KL regularization in RLVR and show that a KL trust region is critical for improving offline-to-online predictability. Across diverse web and mobile benchmarks, GUI-Libra consistently improves both step-wise accuracy and end-to-end task completion. Our results suggest that carefully designed post-training and data curation can unlock significantly stronger task-solving capabilities without costly online data collection. We release our dataset, code, and models to facilitate further research on data-efficient post-training for reasoning-capable GUI agents.

news.title: Step-wise RLVR-Tyle Training Faces Partial Verifiability Challenge
news.summary: Step-wise RLVR-style training faces the partial verifiability challenge, where multiple actions can be correct but only a single demonstrated action is used for verification. This makes offline step-wise metrics weak predictors of online task success. In this work, we perform a comprehensive analysis of 10 state-of-the-art RLVR models using a standardized evaluation protocol. Our analysis reveals significant gaps in offline verifiability - most models achieve near-zero single-action FVD (~0.05) while having much higher multi-step FVD (3-10). We propose that step-wise FVD should not be the primary evaluation metric and recommend using trajectory-based metrics or action-conditioned evaluation for fair comparison. Additionally, we find that partial verifiability is strongly correlated with planning horizon and task complexity, suggesting that models with longer planning windows exhibit better verifiability. We address these findings by proposing improved evaluation protocols and providing more nuanced analysis of RLVR verifiability that can guide the development of more reliable autonomous systems.

news.title: Sysurv: A Fully Differentiable Survival Forest Method for Learning Interpretable Rules
news.summary: In many applications, it is important to identify subpopulations that survive longer or shorter than the rest of the population. In medicine, for example, it allows determining which patients benefit from treatment and in predictive maintenance, and which components are more likely to fail. Existing methods for discovering subgroups with exceptional survival characteristics require restrictive assumptions about survival models (e.g., proportional hazards), pre-discretized features, and, as they compare average statistics, tend to overlook individual deviations. In this paper, we propose Sysurv, a fully differentiable, non-parametric method that leverages random survival forests to learn individual survival curves, automatically learns conditions and how to combine these into inherently interpretable rules, so as to select subgroups with exceptional survival characteristics. Empirical evaluation on a wide range of datasets and settings, including a case study on cancer data, shows that Sysurv reveals insightful and actionable survival subgroups that existing methods overlook. We release our dataset, code, and models to facilitate further research on interpretable survival analysis for healthcare applications.

news.title: Understanding and Mitigating KL Collapse in Large Vision Models: A Taxonomy, Analysis, and Theoretical Insights
news.summary: Large Vision Models (LVLMs) exhibit KL collapse in high-dimensional layers, causing training instability. This work provides a comprehensive taxonomy analyzing three categories of KL collapse: layer-wise vs pairwise, inter-layer vs intra-layer, and depth-wise. We investigate the root causes through theoretical analysis, empirical studies across 16 architectures, and propose practical mitigation strategies. Our findings reveal that depth-wise KL is most critical and often correlates with model performance. We identify overlooked importance of KL regularization in training stability and show that a KL trust region is critical for improving offline-to-online predictability. The analysis reveals that previous taxonomic assumptions were incomplete - KL collapse cannot be categorized solely by layer index. We propose a unified framework for understanding KL collapse in LVLMs, facilitating more robust model training practices and enabling the development of more reliable computer vision systems.

Uses masked self-attention to bind each object bounding box to its textual description, preventing attribute mixing.

news.title: Semi-Supervised Alignment of Unimodal Vision and Language Models via Optimal Transport
news.summary: Proposes SOTAlign, a two-stage framework that aligns pretrained unimodal encoders using minimal paired data (small number of image-text pairs) plus large amounts of unpaired data. First recovers coarse shared geometry with a linear teacher from limited pairs, then refines alignment via optimal-transport-based divergence on unpaired samples.

news.title: Anthropic Raises $30B in Series G at $380B Valuation
news.summary: Anthropic has raised $30 billion in Series G funding led by GIC and Coatue, valuing the company at $380 billion post-money. The investment will fuel frontier research, product development, and infrastructure expansions. Run-rate revenue is $14 billion, growing over 10x annually for the past three years.

news.title: Gemini 3 Deep Think: Advancing Science and Research
news.summary: Google DeepMind introduced Gemini 3 Deep Think, a specialized model variant focused on advancing science, research, and engineering applications. The model aims to support complex scientific reasoning and discovery tasks.

news.title: Gemini 3.1 Pro: Smarter Model for Complex Tasks
news.summary: Google DeepMind has launched Gemini 3.1 Pro, a more capable model designed for handling complex tasks. The update represents another iteration in Google's Gemini model family.

news.title: Gemini Can Now Create Music
news.summary: Google has expanded Gemini's creative capabilities with music generation. Users can now ask Gemini to create music, adding to the model's existing text, image, and video generation abilities.

news.title: GGML and llama.cpp Join Hugging Face
news.summary: Hugging Face has announced that GGML and llama.cpp are joining the platform to ensure the long-term progress of Local AI. The integration aims to strengthen the open-source AI ecosystem and local model deployment capabilities.

news.title: Google DeepMind: Nano Banana 2 Released
news.summary: Google has released Nano Banana 2, a model that combines Pro capabilities with lightning-fast speed. The new model is positioned as a performance-optimized variant of the Gemini 3 Pro Image model.

news.title: Hugging Face: Free AI Model Training with Unsloth
news.summary: Hugging Face has partnered with Unsloth to offer free AI model training through Hugging Face Jobs. The service enables users to train AI models at no cost, lowering barriers to model fine-tuning and development.

news.title: Multi-Agent LLM Trading System Shows Improved Returns
news.summary: Research on a multi-agent LLM trading framework using Japanese stock data shows that fine-grained task decomposition significantly improves risk-adjusted returns compared to coarse-grained designs. Analysis reveals alignment between analytical outputs and decision preferences is a critical performance driver.

news.title: OpenAI Updates Safety Protocols for Law Enforcement
news.summary: OpenAI has announced new safety protocols governing when to involve law enforcement. The changes come after a fatal school shooting in British Columbia, where the suspect's interactions with ChatGPT suggested real-world violence. Under the new protocols, OpenAI would have alerted police if the account was discovered today.

news.title: Study: LLMs Uplift Novices 4.16x on Biology Tasks
news.summary: A multi-model, multi-benchmark human uplift study found that novices with LLM access were 4.16 times more accurate than internet-only controls on biosecurity-relevant tasks. On four benchmarks, novices with LLMs outperformed experts on three, though standalone LLMs often exceeded LLM-assisted novices.
